content.full_text:
Fern Por-rrrcAt- Pnacrrces CoMMrssroN
,128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(9t6) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
May 6, 2010
Gregory V. Moser
Procopio, Cory, Hargreaves & Savitch, LLP
530 B Street, Suite 2100
San Diego, California 9210l-4469
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-10-059
Dear Mr. Moser:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest code
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").'
QT.JESTTON
Is the County of Los Angeles the code reviewing body for the Port of Los Angeles High
School charter school (POLAH), and if so may POLAH maintain its own conflict-of-interest
code or must it be included in the Los Angeles Unified School District's conflict-of-interest
code?
CONCLUSION
The County of Los Angeles is the code reviewing body for the POLAH charter school,
and POLAH must follow the County of Los Angles' determination that POLAH be included in
the Los Angeles Unified School District's conflict-of-interest code.
FACTS
You are an attomey requesting advice on behalf of James Cross, the Executive Director
of POLAH, the Board of Trustees of POLAH and the POLAH charter school. POLAH is
organized as a California non-profit public benefit corporation that has been determined to be
exempt from income taxation under Intemal Revenue Code section 501(cX3). The primary
purpose of the corporation is to operate a tuition-free public high school under the California
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections I 8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 ol the Calitbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-10-059
Page No.2
Charter Schools Act. (Education Code Sections 47600,et seq.) poLAH's corporate offices and
the school program's primary site currently are located in san irea.o in i"r e"g"res County.
Under Education Code Section 47605(d), charter school "admissions may not be
determined according to the place of the pupil." In other words, enrollment for poLAH must be
open to all students who are residents of Caiifornia. lndeed, under Education Code Section
47605(d), if there are more applicants than places for students, admissions must be determined
by random public lottery. POLAH serves communities and enrolls students from outside of the
boundaries of Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD). Generally, when renewal of the
charter for the school is sought, LAUSD is required to renew the charter unless certain factual
findings specified by statute are made. If renewal is denied, poLAH may appeal to the county
Board of Education or the State Board of Education, which could then serve as the authorizer of
the charter school and become responsible for oversight of the school.
In June 2009, LAUSD amended its conflict-of-interest code to add a new disclosure
category for persons "who perform under a charter agreement the duties of any position similar
to that of any designated position on the LAUSD .orifli.rof-interest code.,, poLAH did not
receive notice from LAUSD that it intended to add that provision. when poLAH learned of the
provision, POLAH promptly contacted the Conflicrof-Interest Code Division of the Board of
Supervisors of the County of Los Angeles to indicate that POLAH would be submitting a
conflict-of-interest code for review. county officials indicated that the County would not accept
a separate conflict-of-interest code from POLAH.
ANALYSIS
- 
It is the policy of the Act that conflict-of-interest codes be formulated at the most
decentralized level possible, but withorrt precluding intradepartmental review. Any question of
the.level of a department which should be deemed"ur, "u!"ri"y" shall be resolved by the code
reviewing body' (Section 87301.) Because POLAH has only one charter school and that school
is located entirely within I os Angeles County and its charter has been granted by the LAUSD,
the code r:eviewing body for POLAH is the same as the code reviewing-body for LAUSD, the
s9yntl of Los Angeles. Because the County of Los Angeles is the cod-e reviewing body for
PoLAH its determination that POLAH's conflict-of-intirest code should be included in the
conflict-of-interest code for LAUSD must be followed.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By:
, Attrlug
Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question:
Is the County of Los Angeles the code reviewing body for the Port of Los Angeles High
School charter school (POLAH), and if so may POLAH maintain its own conflict-of-interest
code or must it be included in the Los Angeles Unified School District's conflict-of-interest
code?
sections.conclusion:
The County of Los Angeles is the code reviewing body for the POLAH charter school,
and POLAH must follow the County of Los Angles' determination that POLAH be included in
the Los Angeles Unified School District's conflict-of-interest code.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the County of Los Angeles the code reviewing body for the Port of Los Angeles High
School charter school (POLAH), and if so may POLAH maintain its own conflict-of-interest
code or must it be included in the Los Angeles Unified School District's conflict-of-interest
code?

CONCLUSION: The County of Los Angeles is the code reviewing body for the POLAH charter school,
and POLAH must follow the County of Los Angles' determination that POLAH be included in
the Los Angeles Unified School District's conflict-of-interest code.
